items.title: Materials Science Forum
items.abstract: Materials Science Forum (MSF) is a peer-reviewed journal. The journal scope covers all aspects of theoretical, computational and experimental research in the area of materials science. It includes but is not limited to materials synthesis, analysis of materials properties,the technology of materials processing and materials application. Materials Science Forum is one of the largest periodicals in its field. Materials Science Forum specializes on the publication of thematically complete volumes a

items.title: Energy-Efficiency Theory, Volume I: The Operating Mechanism of the Universe
items.abstract: Abstract This first volume of Energy-Efficiency Theory establishes the ontological foundation for a unified framework that traces the operation of the universe from energy to matter, field, life, and agency. Based on three irreducible axioms—energy as the sole ontology, existence as the result of energy maintenance, and the natural tendency of energy to diffuse from constrained to free states—the book demonstrates that all existences are specific forms of energy under constraints. It introduces

items.title: The Two Fundamental Forms of Energy: Free State and Constrained State — A Physical Ontological Foundation of Energy-Efficiency Theory
items.abstract: This paper systematically expounds the two fundamental forms of energy in Energy-Efficiency Theory (EET): the free state (continuously distributed, propagating at light speed, e.g., waves and fields) and the constrained state (localized, stable, subluminal, e.g., particles and matter). It provides rigorous definitions, mathematical characterizations, and quantitative models of state transitions. Using this framework, it unifies wave-particle duality, quantum measurement collapse, and the mass-en

items.title: The Ontology of Time: The Energy Basis of Irreversibility
items.abstract: An energy-ontological interpretation of time within Energy-Efficiency Theory: elapsed time as ordered energy transitions, irreversibility as constraint rupture and dissipation, with heuristic metrics and operational definitions.

items.title: Quantum Mechanics as Energy-State Transitions: An Energy-Efficiency Interpretation
items.abstract: Quantum mechanics describes microscopic phenomena with extraordinary precision, yet its ontological foundations remain contested. This paper develops a complementary interpretation within Energy-Efficiency Theory (EET). items.title: The Ontology of Phase Transitions: Critical Escape in Energy-Efficiency Theory
items.abstract: Phase transitions—from the freezing of water to the formation of cosmic structures—are among the most ubiquitous phenomena in nature. Yet a unified ontological account of why phase transitions occur, why they exhibit critical slowing down, and how they relate to energy constraints remains lacking. This paper develops an interpretation within Energy-Efficiency Theory (EET). Starting from Yang's Axioms, we propose that phase transitions are critical escape events where the average escape tendency

items.title: Cybersecurity in the quantum era: Assessing the impact of quantum computing on infrastructure

threshold_proximity.evidence: Quantum computing approaching computational capabilities that breach current encryption security thresholds, triggering urgent reassessment of infrastructure vulnerability.

external_pressure.evidence: Geopolitical competition in quantum development and regulatory pressure to secure critical infrastructure against quantum threats creating windows for security standards adoption.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Shift from quantum computing research phase to deployment phase with increasing government and enterprise focus on practical security implications and mitigation strategies.

institutional_change.evidence: Post-quantum cryptography standards being finalized (NIST standardization efforts) and regulatory frameworks for quantum-safe infrastructure being established.

uncertainty_resolution.evidence: Government policies and standards locking in post-quantum cryptography requirements, with major infrastructure operators making irreversible investments in quantum-resistant systems.

items.title: Industrial relations in the privatised coal industry: continuity, change and contradictions
items.abstract: This study seeks both to describe and account for the patterns of industrial relations which have emerged in the UK coal industry since privatisation in 1994. In doing so, it also aims to address some of the wider questions concerning the relationship between ownership and industrial relations. A series of hypotheses are advanced concerning how changes in ownership might affect industrial relations within the industry, and whether such changes would have positive or negative implications for org

external_pressure.evidence: Privatisation of UK coal industry in 1994 represents a major geopolitical and regulatory restructuring event that fundamentally altered industrial relations frameworks.

institutional_change.evidence: Privatisation created new institutional arrangements and regulatory structures governing labour relations in the coal sector, marking a shift from state-owned to private sector governance.

Towards a framework for cross-boundary collaborative open learning for cross-institutional academic development
items.abstract: This phenomenographic study, explores the collaborative open learning experience of academic staff and open learners in cross-institutional academic development settings, and adds to what is known in these settings. It provides new insights for academic developers and course designers about the benefits of crossing boundaries (i.e. open learning) in an academic development context and proposes an alternative model to traditional academic Continuing Professional Development (CPD). It engages acad

external_pressure.evidence: Open learning and cross-institutional collaboration reflect broader pressure toward democratized education and institutional boundary dissolution in response to digital transformation and accessibility demands.

institutional_change.evidence: The study examines emerging frameworks for cross-institutional academic development, suggesting institutional structures and practices are being reconfigured to enable collaborative open learning models.

items.title: DSFB Structural Semiotics Engine for Battery Health Monitoring: A Deterministic Early-Warning Framework for Capacity Fade, Internal Resistance Drift, and Knee-Onset Detection in Safety-Critical Energy Storage Systems
items.abstract: We present a deterministic residual-structure signal for lithium-ion battery health monitoringand evaluate it on the NASA PCoE battery aging dataset (cell B0005). The signal indicates atransition region at cycle 38, compared to cycle 79 for a standard 85% capacity threshold andcycle 101 for end-of-life (80%), corresponding to a 41-cycle lead relative to threshold crossing.The signal remains elevated over multiple cycles, indicating sustained structural change ratherthan a transient fluctuation.

threshold_proximity.evidence: Research focuses on detecting critical transition points (knee-onset, capacity fade thresholds) in battery performance metrics, indicating proximity to inflection points in battery degradation curves.

external_pressure.evidence: Safety-critical energy storage systems face regulatory and safety pressures as grid-scale and EV battery deployments expand, creating demand for early-warning frameworks.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Shift from fundamental battery research toward deployment-phase monitoring solutions suggests movement from research to practical implementation in operational systems.

items.title: Journal of Communication and Information Systems
items.abstract: With the increasing tendency of incorporating technology into environments and activities in everyday life, new methods are being proposed to better integrate devices and mankind.Networks designed for monitoring areas through video and image systems are being implemented in several applications.Recent studies have shown interest from both the academy and industry to integrate wireless surveillance networks with lowcost and long range transmission technologies, such as LoRa (Long Range).With the

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Academic journal article indicates ongoing research phase in human-technology integration and network design, suggesting technology still in exploration rather than deployment phase.

items.title: Critical evaluation of the battery electric vehicle for sustainable mobility
items.abstract: Can Battery Electric Vehicles replace conventional internal combustion engine vehicles for commuting purposes when exposed to a busy corporate activity within the city of Edinburgh? This thesis investigates the application of Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) use in a commercial business environment in the city of Edinburgh, Scotland UK. The motivation behind this work is to determine if the Battery Electric Vehicle can replace conventional fossil fuel vehicles under real world drive cycles and th

threshold_proximity.evidence: Research evaluating whether BEVs can replace ICE vehicles for commuting suggests the technology is approaching practical viability thresholds for real-world urban deployment scenarios.

external_pressure.evidence: Focus on sustainable mobility and urban commuting in Edinburgh reflects regulatory and social pressure to transition away from conventional combustion engines in cities.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Academic research in 2026 examining practical BEV deployment for corporate commuting indicates the technology is transitioning from research/development toward real-world implementation phase.

uncertainty_resolution.evidence: Empirical evaluation of BEV performance in actual busy corporate urban environments directly addresses key uncertainties about practical viability and real-world adoption feasibility.

items.title: Reliable electricity to advance quality health care in low-resource settings
items.abstract: Reliable, affordable, sustainable, and equitable electricity access in health-care facilities is fundamental to achieving universal health coverage. Decentralised renewable energy systems coupled with battery storage present a key opportunity to rapidly expand access to reliable electricity, particularly for remote facilities where grid connection is unavailable and as a backup for facilities with unreliable power. In this Series paper, we discuss several measures and policy options for accelera

threshold_proximity.evidence: Healthcare facility electrification in low-resource settings represents a critical performance threshold for achieving universal health coverage, with decentralized renewable systems approaching cost-competitiveness.

external_pressure.evidence: Global health equity imperatives and SDG commitments create regulatory and social pressure for reliable electricity access in underserved healthcare facilities.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Decentralized renewable energy systems are transitioning from research/pilot phase toward deployment in healthcare contexts, indicating maturation in the technology cycle.

institutional_change.evidence: Universal health coverage frameworks and international health standards increasingly mandate reliable electricity access, driving institutional and regulatory alignment.

items.title: Temperature distribution models for double-glazed windows and their use in assessing condensation occurrence.
items.abstract: Condensation might be described as the modem disease of buildings.In dwellings, condensation on window glazing may obstruct the view and, if it becomes so excessive and no means for drainage is provided within the window frame, condensed water will run off causing damage to woodwork, furniture and decorations.The present research contributes to the furtherance of understanding of the temperature distribution in double glazed windows, the mechanism behind such variations and their effect on conde

threshold_proximity.evidence: Research on temperature distribution models suggests condensation occurrence is approaching critical thresholds in building performance, indicating a performance benchmark being breached in modern construction.

external_pressure.evidence: Framing condensation as a 'modern disease of buildings' reflects growing social and regulatory pressure around indoor air quality, moisture control, and building habitability standards.

institutional_change.evidence: Academic modeling of condensation assessment suggests emerging standardization efforts around window performance metrics and building codes addressing moisture management requirements.

items.title: Engineering non-interfacial hydrogen spillover in a Ni17W3-WO2 heterostructure
items.abstract: The hydrogen spillover effect offers a promising strategy to overcome the kinetic bottleneck of proton desorption in hydrogen evolution reaction catalysts. However, conventional hydrogen spillover mechanisms rely on interfacial proton transfer between distinct phases and suffer from inherent energy barriers. threshold_proximity.evidence: Research targeting kinetic bottlenecks in hydrogen evolution reaction suggests the field is approaching performance thresholds where catalytic efficiency gains become commercially viable.

external_pressure.evidence: Global decarbonization goals and hydrogen economy development create sustained pressure for breakthrough catalytic materials to enable cost-competitive green hydrogen production.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Academic publication on novel heterostructure engineering indicates transition from fundamental research toward applied catalyst development for hydrogen production systems.

items.title: Multifunctional Design of Solid Polymer Electrolytes: Paving the Way for High‐Performance All‐Solid‐State Batteries
items.abstract: ABSTRACT All‐solid‐state batteries (ASSBs) have emerged as a pivotal direction in next‐generation energy storage, driven by their compelling potential for enhanced safety and superior energy density. Among the key enabling materials, solid polymer electrolytes (SPEs) stand out due to their structural tunability, manufacturing scalability, and robust interfacial contact, offering a viable pathway toward practical ASSBs. This review systematically bridges the gap between molecular‐level design and

threshold_proximity.evidence: ASSBs are described as 'pivotal' with 'compelling potential' for enhanced safety and superior energy density, suggesting performance benchmarks are being approached or breached in solid electrolyte design.

external_pressure.evidence: Global energy transition and decarbonization imperatives are creating demand for next-generation energy storage solutions with superior safety and density characteristics.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: The framing of ASSBs as 'next-generation' and 'pivotal direction' with focus on multifunctional design optimization indicates transition from research exploration toward deployment-phase engineering challenges.

items.title: Report on reproducibility in condensed matter physics
items.abstract: This report from a diverse collaboration of experts addresses community concerns around reproducibility in condensed matter physics by establishing clear best practices for researchers, publishers, and institutions. It advocates for mandatory sharing of primary data, code, and full experimental parameters to enhance and safeguard the field's scientific integrity.

external_pressure.evidence: Community concerns around reproducibility indicate accumulated pressure on research practices that necessitates coordinated expert response.

institutional_change.evidence: Establishment of clear best practices for researchers, publishers, and institutions represents formalization of standards and governance mechanisms.

items.title: Super-Electron-Donor Polymers: From Monomer Design to Redox Functionality
items.abstract: The design flexibility of organic materials has enabled numerous applications for energy storage systems. However, few examples of low-potential p-type materials for the negative electrode are known in the field of organic batteries, particularly relevant for anion-rocking-chair full-cells. Herein, we present a synthetic design to incorporate bridged 2,2’‑bipyridinium units, which in their reduced form are known as super-electron-donors, into a polymer structure. By adapting their synthesis, we

threshold_proximity.evidence: Research targeting low-potential p-type materials for negative electrodes suggests addressing a critical performance gap in energy storage systems where few viable solutions currently exist.

external_pressure.evidence: Growing demand for advanced energy storage systems driven by electrification and renewable energy integration creates pressure for novel electrode materials beyond conventional options.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Academic publication on monomer design and redox functionality indicates transition from fundamental research toward applied development of practical energy storage materials.

items.title: Life cycle assessment of multi-glazed windows
items.abstract: In 1987 the World Commission on Environment and Development proposed a reduction in per capita energy consumption of 50%. Increasing demands, and initiatives of this nature, produce a need for more reliable assessment methods, measurement tools and improvement regimes. Since the late 1960's Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) has become an increasingly important tool for engineers, technologists, scientists, designers, managers and environmentalists alike. LCA enables the effects which products, process

threshold_proximity.evidence: The 1987 WCED proposal for 50% per capita energy reduction represents a quantified performance benchmark that drives technological innovation in building envelope efficiency.

external_pressure.evidence: International environmental initiatives and increasing regulatory demands for energy consumption reduction create institutional pressure for building technology improvements.

institutional_change.evidence: The World Commission on Environment and Development's formal proposals indicate emerging regulatory frameworks and policy initiatives targeting energy consumption standards.

items.title: Physiochemical Properties Investigation of Thermal–Moisture-Aged Low Voltage PVC Cable Insulation
items.abstract: This study investigates the combined effects of thermal and moisture aging on PVC-insulated low voltage (LV) photovoltaic (PV) cables using an accelerated-aging design to represent realistic PV operating conditions commonly encountered in hot and humid climates. Thermal aging was carried out at 90 °C for five aging cycles, with each thermal cycle followed by controlled moisture injection to simulate moisture stress. The degradation behavior was evaluated using broadband dielectric spectroscopy,

threshold_proximity.evidence: Study investigates critical failure points of PVC cable insulation under combined thermal-moisture stress, identifying performance degradation thresholds relevant to PV system longevity and safety standards.

external_pressure.evidence: Rapid deployment of PV infrastructure globally creates urgent need to understand cable material durability and failure modes in field conditions, driven by renewable energy transition pressures.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Research represents transition from PV module optimization to systems-level reliability engineering, addressing practical deployment challenges as solar capacity scales.

institutional_change.evidence: Accelerated-aging methodology and material property investigation directly supports development of cable certification standards and safety regulations for long-term PV system performance.

items.title: The SEEDZ Simulations: Methodology and First Results on Massive Black Hole Seeding and Early Galaxy Growth
items.abstract: Here we introduce the SEEDZ simulations, a suite of cosmological hydrodynamic simulations exploring the formation and growth of the first massive black holes in the Universe. SEEDZ includes models for Population III star formation, supernovae explosions and the resulting formation of light seed black holes, metal enrichment and subsequent Population II star formation, heavy seed black hole formation, Eddington and super-Eddington accretion schemes as well as black hole feedback. items.title: Eco-Friendly Bioinspired Synthesis and Environmental Applications of Zinc Oxide Nanoparticles Mediated by Natural Polysaccharide Gums: A Sustainable Approach to Nanomaterials Fabrication
items.abstract: The green synthesis of nanomaterials has emerged as a sustainable and environmentally friendly approach, gaining significant attention in recent years for its potential in a wide range of multifunctional applications. Among these materials, zinc oxide nanoparticles (ZnO NPs) stand out due to their remarkable versatility and effectiveness in fields such as industry (food, chemistry, and cosmetics), nanomedicine, cancer therapy, drug delivery, optoelectronics, sensors, and environmental remediatio

external_pressure.evidence: Growing regulatory and social pressure for sustainable manufacturing practices is driving research into environmentally friendly nanomaterial synthesis methods.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Academic publication indicates transition from exploratory research toward applied development phase of green nanotechnology synthesis methods.

items.title: Continuous Snow-Cover Monitoring and Avalanche Detection with a Novel Sensor Array Box
items.abstract: Snow avalanches pose a serious hazard in snow-covered, mountainous areas. In order to protect inhabited areas and infrastructure such as roads and railway lines, avalanche protection measures need to be taken. In addition to permanent, technical protection measures, temporary, organizational measures, which are based on risk assessments by local avalanche warning commissions, are utilized. These avalanche risk assessments rely on regional avalanche bulletins, weather forecasts, local expertise,

threshold_proximity.evidence: Novel sensor array technology suggests performance benchmarks in avalanche detection accuracy and real-time monitoring capabilities are being approached or breached.

external_pressure.evidence: Climate change intensifying snow hazards in mountainous regions creates urgent pressure to develop advanced monitoring systems for protecting inhabited areas and critical infrastructure.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Academic publication of novel sensor array indicates transition from research phase toward deployment-ready technology for avalanche detection systems.

items.title: Enhanced power management in PV-Integrated hybrid energy storage systems using fuzzy 2DOF-PI control optimized by hippopotamus algorithm
items.abstract: This study presents an advanced control strategy for a standalone photovoltaic (PV) system integrated with a hybrid energy storage system (HESS) comprising batteries and supercapacitors (SCs). The proposed system employs a novel Fuzzy Logic-based Two-Degree-of-Freedom Proportional-Integral (Fuzzy 2DOF-PI) controller, optimized using the Hippopotamus Optimization (HO) algorithm, to enhance power management and stability. The batteries address long-term energy demands, while SCs handle instantaneo

threshold_proximity.evidence: Advanced control optimization for PV-HESS systems suggests performance benchmarks and efficiency thresholds are being actively targeted to improve system viability.

external_pressure.evidence: Growing focus on standalone PV systems with hybrid storage reflects regulatory and market pressure to transition away from grid-dependent energy infrastructure.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Research moving from theoretical control algorithms to practical optimization (fuzzy logic, metaheuristic algorithms) indicates shift from research toward deployment phase of energy storage technology.

items.title: U.S. LNG Exports: Truth and Consequence
items.abstract: A decade ago, market players were making large capital investments to facilitate the import to the United States of liquefied natural gas (LNG) from distant locations, such as the Middle East, Africa, and Russia. This was predicted on the consensus at the time that U.S. domestic supply was becoming increasingly scarce. However, innovations involving hydraulic fracturing and horizontal drilling subsequently let to the dramatic growth of domestic production from shale gas. In fact, domestic produc

threshold_proximity.evidence: Decade-long shift from LNG import infrastructure to export capacity represents crossing a critical threshold in U.S. energy market positioning and self-sufficiency metrics.

external_pressure.evidence: Geopolitical realignment and changing energy security concerns shifted U.S. strategy from importing LNG from Middle East and Africa to becoming an exporter.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Large capital investments in LNG infrastructure indicate movement from research/pilot phase to large-scale deployment and commercialization phase.

institutional_change.evidence: Shift in U.S. energy policy and regulatory framework enabled transition from import-focused to export-focused LNG infrastructure development.

uncertainty_resolution.evidence: Large-scale capital commitments to LNG export facilities represent irreversible investments that lock in the U.S. export strategy and resolve prior uncertainty about energy market direction.

items.title: Quantum-classical generative models for drug design
items.abstract: Abstract In molecular research, the modelling and analysis of molecules through simulation is an important part that has a direct influence on medical development, material science and drug discovery. The processing power required to design protein chains with hundreds of peptides is huge. Classical computing techniques, including state-of-the-art machine learning models being deployed on classical computing machines, have proven to be inefficient in this task, though they have been successful i

threshold_proximity.evidence: Quantum-classical hybrid models represent a critical threshold where quantum computing begins demonstrating practical utility in real-world drug discovery applications, moving beyond theoretical advantage.

external_pressure.evidence: Pharmaceutical industry faces pressure to accelerate drug discovery timelines and reduce R&D costs, creating demand for computational breakthroughs in molecular modeling.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Shift from pure quantum research toward hybrid quantum-classical deployment models indicates transition from research phase to practical application phase with commercial relevance.

items.title: Predicting mosquito flight behavior using Bayesian dynamical systems learning
items.abstract: Mosquito-borne diseases cause several hundred thousand deaths worldwide every year. Deciphering mosquito host-seeking behavior is essential to prevent disease transmission through mosquito capture and surveillance. Despite recent substantial progress, we still lack a comprehensive quantitative understanding of how visual and other sensory cues guide mosquitoes to their targets. Here, we combined three-dimensional infrared tracking of Aedes aegypti mosquitoes with Bayesian dynamical system

external_pressure.evidence: Mosquito-borne diseases causing hundreds of thousands of deaths annually creates sustained external pressure for novel intervention technologies and control methods.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Application of Bayesian dynamical systems and machine learning to mosquito behavior represents transition from theoretical research to practical deployment phase for disease vector control.

items.title: Beyond access: clean energy use in low-income and middle-income countries
items.abstract: Access to clean energy-here defined as electricity, liquefied petroleum gas, biogas, and ethanol-has increased substantially in low-income and middle-income countries over the past three decades. However, millions still lack reliable and affordable access to electricity and clean cooking fuels. This Series paper explores the drivers of clean energy adoption, assesses tools for tracking progress, and examines persistent barriers-including high costs, unreliable supply, and insufficient availabili

threshold_proximity.evidence: Substantial increase in clean energy access over three decades suggests crossing critical adoption thresholds in LMICs, moving beyond initial penetration phases.

external_pressure.evidence: Global climate commitments and SDG targets create regulatory and social pressure for clean energy deployment in low-income and middle-income countries.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Shift from access-focused research to use-focused analysis indicates maturation from deployment phase toward optimization and scaling of clean energy technologies.

institutional_change.evidence: Widespread clean energy access expansion implies establishment of regulatory frameworks, certification systems, and standards for electricity and fuel distribution in LMICs.

items.title: A complete set of canonical nucleobases in the carbonaceous asteroid (162173) Ryugu
items.abstract: Abstract Organic molecules delivered from extraterrestrial materials may have played a key role in supplying building blocks for life on Earth. Here we report all five canonical nucleobases—purines (adenine and guanine) and pyrimidines (cytosine, thymine and uracil)—in samples returned from the C-type asteroid (162173) Ryugu by JAXA’s Hayabusa2 mission and compare the results with data from similar extraterrestrial material. Ryugu samples contain nearly equal amounts of purines and pyrimidines,

threshold_proximity.evidence: Discovery of all five canonical nucleobases in Ryugu samples crosses a critical threshold in validating the panspermia hypothesis and demonstrating that complex organic chemistry can occur in extraterrestrial environments.

external_pressure.evidence: Japan's Hayabusa2 sample-return mission and international interest in understanding life's origins create geopolitical and scientific momentum for astrobiology research.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: This represents a shift from theoretical modeling to empirical validation through advanced analytical techniques applied to actual extraterrestrial samples, marking deployment of space-based sample analysis capabilities.

uncertainty_resolution.evidence: The definitive detection of all five nucleobases resolves long-standing uncertainty about whether prebiotic chemistry could produce life's molecular building blocks in space, reducing a major competing hypothesis uncertainty.

items.title: Dogs were widely distributed across western Eurasia during the Palaeolithic
items.abstract: Archaeological evidence suggests that dogs diverged from wolves during the Palaeolithic, more than 15,000 years ago<sup>1-7</sup>. The earliest unequivocal genetic evidence, however, is associated with dog remains from Mesolithic archaeological contexts approximately 10,900 years ago<sup>8,9</sup>. items.title: Open and sustainable AI: challenges, opportunities and the road ahead in the life sciences
items.abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) has seen transformative breakthroughs in the life sciences, expanding possibilities to interpret biological information at an unprecedented capacity. To maximize return on growing investments and accelerate progress, it is urgent to address long-standing research challenges arising from the rapid adoption of AI methods. We review the erosion of trust in AI outputs driven by poor reusability and reproducibility, and highlight their impact on environmental sustainabili

threshold_proximity.evidence: AI achieving 'transformative breakthroughs' and 'unprecedented capacity' in biological interpretation suggests performance metrics crossing critical capability thresholds in life sciences applications.

external_pressure.evidence: Emphasis on 'open and sustainable AI' indicates regulatory and social pressure for responsible AI governance in high-stakes life sciences domain.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: Discussion of maximizing 'return on' investment suggests transition from research phase toward deployment and commercialization of AI in life sciences.

institutional_change.evidence: Focus on 'open' and 'sustainable' frameworks indicates emerging standards, certification systems, and institutional governance structures being established for AI in life sciences.

items.title: MOJ Food Processing & Technology
items.abstract: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) represents one of the most pressing global health threats, primarily driven by the overuse and misuse of antibiotics in healthcare, animal farming, and other sectors.The rapid emergence of multidrug-resistant (MDR) pathogens has rendered many conventional antibiotics ineffective, creating an urgent need for alternative therapeutic strategies.In this context, the exploration of phytochemicals offers a promising and sustainable approach.These naturally occurring plan

threshold_proximity.evidence: AMR is described as 'one of the most pressing global health threats,' indicating a critical threshold has been breached in disease resistance metrics.

external_pressure.evidence: Overuse of antibiotics in healthcare and animal farming creates regulatory and social pressure to develop alternative food processing technologies.

items.title: Spectral Uniformity of Little Red Dots: A Natural Outcome of Coevolving Seed Black Holes and Nascent Starbursts
items.abstract: Abstract The birth of seeds of massive black holes (BHs) and nascent galaxies at cosmic dawn takes place in dense gaseous environments, which play a crucial role in shaping their coevolution and radiation spectra. We investigate gas accretion during the assembly of massive halos with M h ≳ 10 10 −10 11 M ⊙ at redshifts z ≃ 4–10, driving both rapid BH feeding and concurrent nuclear starbursts. items.title: Machine-learning enhanced simulations predict graphene is hydrophobic and microscopically not wetting transparent
items.abstract: The interaction between graphene and water is fundamental to applications ranging from filtration to nano-electronics, yet the intrinsic wettability of graphene remains a subject of longstanding debate. In particular, it is disputed whether graphene is wetting transparent-transmitting the wettability of an underlying substrate to the surface. Here, we show using machine-learning enhanced molecular dynamics simulations that pristine graphene is intrinsically hydrophobic and microscopically not we

threshold_proximity.evidence: Resolution of longstanding debate about graphene's intrinsic wettability represents crossing a critical knowledge threshold that unlocks design parameters for filtration and nano-electronics applications.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: ML-enhanced simulations indicate shift from theoretical research to computational validation phase, enabling transition from fundamental science to application-ready material characterization.

uncertainty_resolution.evidence: Definitive computational prediction of graphene's hydrophobic properties resolves a fundamental uncertainty that has constrained material selection decisions in filtration and electronics applications.

items.title: Primordial Black Holes as Seeds for Extremely Overmassive Active Galactic Nuclei Observed by JWST
items.abstract: Abstract The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) has recently identified A2744–QSO1 as a compact, metal-poor, black hole (BH)–dominated galaxy at z ≃ 7. This system exhibits an extreme black-hole-to-stellar mass ratio and unusually low metallicity, posing significant challenges to BH seeding models. Motivated by these discoveries, we perform high-resolution cosmological simulations with a massive primordial black hole (PBH; M BH = 5 × 10 7 M ⊙ ) seed, incorporating for the first time a fully coupl

threshold_proximity.evidence: JWST observations of z≃7 black holes challenge theoretical models of black hole formation timescales, suggesting critical thresholds in early universe black hole growth mechanisms are being breached.

tech_cycle_position.evidence: JWST deployment represents transition from research/development phase to operational discovery phase, enabling new observational capabilities that resolve previously inaccessible cosmic phenomena.

uncertainty_resolution.evidence: Direct observational evidence from JWST resolves long-standing uncertainty about primordial black hole formation mechanisms and early universe black hole seeding, eliminating competing theoretical models.

items.title: Sonicated Mucilage from Defatted Flaxseed Meal as a Sustainable Natural Gum: Functional and Thermal Characterization
items.abstract: Flaxseed mucilage mainly consists of soluble fibers in the outermost layers of the seed. Because of its unique functional properties, such as water holding capacity, water absorption capacity, oil holding capacity and emulsion activity, it can be used as a natural gum in the food industry. Flaxseed mucilage can also exhibit certain health benefits, including anti-obesity, anti-diabetes, anti-cholesterol and antioxidant activities. external_pressure.evidence: Growing regulatory and consumer pressure to replace synthetic additives with sustainable natural alternatives is driving exploration of plant-based gum substitutes.

items.title: Remembering the opponent: neuronal activation associated with social memory in zebrafish
items.abstract: Learning and remembering an opponent and the characteristics of a previous encounter may allow individuals to modify their behavior based on that acquired information. In the present study, using a long-term memory paradigm associated with social interactions, we aimed to evaluate the neuronal activation underlying individual recognition and memory of a previous agonistic encounter in male zebrafish.
